addVoiceHint: The SID must be registered as a member of the incarnation (coven = incarnation.name).
allSoulsAreVoices: All incarnation hosts are already added as Voices.
choirDescriptionPlaceholder: Purpose of the partition, e.g. primary nodes
choirMaxLtMin: max_size cannot be less than min_size.
choirNameHint: kebab/snake: starts with a letter, only lowercase letters, digits, _ and -.
choirsDesc: Choir — a named host topology within an incarnation (ADR-044). A single host can be a Voice in multiple Choirs.
choirsEmpty: No Choirs. Create the first one with «Create Choir».
choirNoVoices: No Voices in this Choir. Add the first one.
deleteChoirConfirmLabel: I understand — delete the Choir together with all its Voices.
deleteChoirDesc: Choir {{choir}} will be deleted along with its Voices (ON DELETE CASCADE).
deleteChoirWarningBody: Deleting Choir {{choir}} is irreversible: all Voices (SID memberships) will be deleted via cascade. Managed hosts are not affected.
voiceNotMemberError: SID is not a member of the incarnation — assign coven={{name}} to it in the Souls registry first.
choirsDegradedLead: Choir API is currently unavailable (backend returned {{status}}). The subsystem is disabled or an older version of Keeper is in use.
choirsDegradedHint: Once the choir subsystem becomes available, the list of Choirs will appear here automatically.
addHostDesc: Add a host to declared spec.hosts[] (mode=append). If the SID matches, role is updated.
addHostHint: Add a host with the «Add host» button.
addHostUnknownSid: Unknown SID — host is not registered in the souls registry. {{detail}}
filterCovenMultiEmpty: No coven labels in the loaded set.
filterTraitsMultiEmpty: No traits in the loaded set.
runSetAria: Run a scenario on the filtered set of incarnations
stateFilterNoFields: The state schema contains no declared fields.
stateFilterSchemaUnavailable: State schema unavailable — filtering by fields is not possible.
stateFilterSelectService: select a service to filter by state fields
allDeclaredSouls: All registered souls are already in the declared list.
allowDestroyDesc: — teardown without a teardown-scenario (force, DELETE the row directly). Without the flag a regular destroy runs via scenario
connectedSoulsDesc: The match with reality (declared ↔ connected) can be checked via a probe-scenario; this is a derived view, not authoritative.
covenInvalid: Invalid coven label (expected lowercase, digits and a dash as separator).
covensPlaceholder: prod, datacenter-1 (Enter / space / comma to add)
createNoInput: The selected scenario create has no input fields — the incarnation is created without input parameters.
createBareInfo: This service offers no start scenarios — a bare incarnation will be created; operations run via day-2 scenarios.
createFromSoulsHint: This scenario requires already onboarded souls in coven «{{coven}}». Add them via the Hosts tab on the incarnation before running.
dataSummaryStateTail: — what the system knows after apply runs
declaredHostsDesc: Declared list of hosts from incarnation.spec.hosts[]. Source of truth for bootstrap-create and the topology resolver.
destroyIrreversible: This action is irreversible. Async operation: incarnation transitions to
dynamicInputHint: Passed as input to scenario create. Empty = {}.
forceAddConfirmLabel: I understand the risk — force-add the host.
forceAddNotConfirmed: Confirm the force-add by checking the box.
forceAddWarningBody: Force-adding a host may lead to an inconsistent state across the group of Souls. The host does not go through the reconciliation scenario (add-scenario). Are you sure?
inputComplexJson: The scenario schema contains complex types — JSON mode.
listEmptyLead: No incarnations matched the filter. Click
migrationGrammarHint: See the migration grammar (rename / set / delete / move + foreach) in the service repository files. Upgrade to a new version — via the «Upgrade» action in the header (forward-only, atomic in a single PG transaction, snapshot in state_history).
migrationsEmpty: No migrations — the service is on the first version of the state structure.
noHostsInState: No data in state.hosts — either there was no apply, or the scenario does not write per-host state.
perHostDataDesc: Convention: the scenario writes per-host state to
perHostRuntimeDesc: Per-host subsection incarnation.state.hosts[<sid>] — convention: the scenario writes per-host state into this section (role, pid, users, etc.). If the scenario does not use the convention — the section is empty.
removeBlocked409: Incarnation is in destroying state — editing spec.hosts is not possible.
removeHostConfirmLabel: I understand the consequences — remove the host from the declared list.
removeHostDesc: The host is removed from the incarnation's declared spec.hosts[]. The Soul itself and its coven binding are untouched — only the operator's declared list changes.
removeHostNotConfirmed: Confirm the removal by checking the box.
removeHostWarningBody: Removing host {{sid}} from the declared list of incarnation {{name}} is irreversible: on the next run this host will no longer be in the spec, and scenarios will stop accounting for it. Are you sure?
roleKebab: Role — kebab-case (lowercase, digits, dash separator), up to 63 characters.
runAcceptedTail: . History will update once the run completes.
scenarioCreateAlwaysHint: POST /v1/incarnations always calls scenario create; the choice here sets the input_schema for field generation.
scenarioUnavailable: Scenario catalog is unavailable. The name is entered manually; the match with scenario/<name>/ in the service is verified by Keeper.
schemaDegradedLead: Detailed state-schema for this service is currently unavailable (backend returned {{status}}). Structure and migrations:
schemaDegradedMigrationsHint: the migrations/<NNN>_to_<MMM>.yml directory in the service repository.
schemaDegradedStructHint: the state_schema_version: {{version}} field and the optional state_schema: declaration in service.yml of service {{service}} at ref {{ref}}.
schemaNotDeclared: State structure is not declared in service.yml (state_schema: is absent). State fields are determined dynamically by scenarios.
servicesLoadFailed: Failed to load services. POST /v1/incarnations will validate the name server-side.
specEmpty: spec is not set — incarnation created without declared parameters.
specHostsNotSet: spec.hosts is not set. The volatile role is determined by a probe step in the scenario (core.exec.run + register: + where:).
specSourceDesc: Source: incarnation.spec — what the operator declared when creating the incarnation (the create scenario input and/or spec.hosts[]). Read-only.
stateEmpty: state is empty — the incarnation has not gone through apply scenarios yet, or the scenarios wrote nothing into state.
stateSourceLead: Source: incarnation.state — written by the scenario applier after successful apply runs. Read-only.
stateSourceTail: — the structure is defined in service.yml (see the Schema tab).
title: New incarnation
toVersionRequired: specify the git-ref of the target version
rerunLastDesc: Atomically removes error_locked and reruns the last failed scenario (create or day-2) with that run's saved input. Reason is required — it is written to the audit log.
rerunLastReasonPlaceholder: What was fixed manually for the rerun?
rerunLast409: Cannot rerun: the incarnation is not in error_locked status. Refresh the page.
rerunLastInputUnavailable: The failed run's input is unavailable (cleaned up by retention) — remove the lock with a regular Unlock and run the scenario manually with explicit input.
rerunLastTooltip: Atomic unlock + rerun of the last failed scenario (error_locked → applying). Equivalent to Unlock + manual Run, but without a race window.
unlockDesc: The operator takes responsibility for host consistency. The reason is written to state_history.metadata.unlock_reason.
upgradeDesc: Runs a state migration + switches service_version in a single PG transaction. Current binding:
upgradeModeFoundHint: The target version has an upgrade scenario — host orchestration runs automatically.
upgradeModeLegacyHint: The target version has no upgrade scenario — the version switch causes drift, finished by a normal apply.
traitsHint: key → scalar value or list of values. Projected into souls.traits of member hosts.
editTraitsIntro: Full replacement of incarnation.traits (source of truth). The form is pre-filled with current values: remove a row to delete a trait. Member hosts' traits will be overwritten.
provisionDisabledHint: The run will use already-onboarded souls in coven «{{coven}}». Add them via the Hosts tab on the incarnation before running.
provisionHostWarningBody: This run needs {{n}} host(s). Enable cloud provision (creates VMs automatically) OR onboard the required number of souls to coven «{{coven}}» via Hosts before running.
